CONTENT.Honours: GranadaSegunda División: 2022–23IndividualUruguayan Primera División Team of the Year: 2020, 2023
CONTENT.Club career: An academy graduate of River Plate Montevideo, Arezo made his professional debut on 14 July 2019 in a 0–0 draw against Progreso. He scored his first professional goal on 10 August 2019, in a 2–1 league win against Juventud.On 31 January 2022, Spanish club Granada announced the signing of Arezo on a four-and-a-half-year deal. On 20 January 2023, he returned to Uruguayan league by signing for Peñarol on a loan deal until the end of the year.On 18 July 2024, Arezo joined Brazilian club Grêmio on a four-and-a-half-year contract until December 2028.
CONTENT.Career statistics: As of match played 15 May 2024As of 20 June 2023Scores and results list Uruguay's goal tally first, score column indicates score after each Arezo goal.
CONTENT.International career: Arezo is a former Uruguay youth international and has represented his nation at the 2017 South American U-15 Championship and the 2019 South American U-17 Championship. He scored five goals including a hat-trick against Ecuador in the latter tournament and finished second in top scorers list.In March 2023, Arezo received his first call-up to the senior team for friendly matches against Japan and South Korea.

SUMMARY: Douglas Matías Arezo Martínez (born 21 November 2002) is a Uruguayan professional footballer who plays as a striker for Campeonato Brasileiro Série A club Grêmio and the Uruguay national team.
